LHG1-W6Z: Hugh Hext was born in Dorset, England, in 1650 and lived through a time of great migration across the Atlantic. In 1687 he sailed from Weymouth in his native Dorset, immigrating to the New World, and he eventually made his home in Christ Church Parish near Charleston, South Carolina, where he died in 1732 at the age of about eighty-two.
LZXY-CGP: Nicolas Sarrazin was born in 1655 in the Seine region of France and crossed the ocean to settle in New France. He died in 1700 at Petite Auvergne in Charlesbourg, Quebec, having lived out his later years in the young colony along the St. Lawrence.
LHGK-K13: Marie Catherine Blondeau was born in 1664 at Château-Richer, one of the early settlements of New France, and made her home in the colony along the St. Lawrence. She lived for a time at Beauport near Quebec City before settling in Charlesbourg, where she died in 1728.
LYJQ-V89: Nicole Roland was born in 1634 in the parish of Saint-Sulpice in Paris, France. Around 1655 she emigrated to New France as a fille à marier, one of the marriageable young women who helped build the fledgling colony, and she settled at Charlesbourg in Quebec, where she died in 1694.
LZG4-GXY: Margaret Bernard was born in 1624 in Kingsthorpe, Northamptonshire, England. In 1655 she immigrated across the Atlantic to Virginia, where she settled in Old Rappahannock and lived out the rest of her life, dying there in 1687.
LZ9C-MDB: William Weeks was born in 1645 in Falmouth, then part of Plymouth Colony, and spent his life in the same community as it grew into Barnstable County, Massachusetts. He died in Falmouth in 1716 at about seventy-one years of age.
LJRV-294: Robert H. Jeter was born in 1656 in Suffolk, England, and lived his entire life there, dying in the same county in 1736 at the age of about eighty.
KZZR-C3S: Isaac Jauslin was baptized on 24 June 1638 in the Swiss town of Sissach, in the canton of Basel. He made his home in Sissach in Basel-Landschaft, where the records of his life are rooted.
KNQZ-C1S: Sir John Webb was the 3rd Baronet Webb of Odstock House, an English nobleman whose life is recorded in published biography. He died in 1745 at Aachen, which at that time was known as Aix-la-Chapelle and lay within France.
L413-3DY: Elisabetha Adam was born in 1624 and lived in the German lands of the Rhineland-Palatinate region. She died in 1693 at Rathsweiler, near Kusel, at the age of about sixty-nine.
LZD8-M8Z: Jean Baptiste Haimon II was born in 1698 in Canada and worked as a miller at the far end of the island of Montreal. He later crossed the Atlantic to France, where he died in 1750 at Vroil in the Marne.
L6H6-HGL: Mrs. Hammond lived in colonial Maryland and was connected to the Hammond and Ford families. She was part of the team that laid out the city of Annapolis, contributing to the design of the new colonial capital, and she died in Anne Arundel County in 1678.
GL9P-3GL: Nicholas Edmunds was born in 1710 in Surry, Virginia, and rose to public office in his community. By 1750 he served as sheriff of Brunswick County, Virginia, where he later died in 1787 at the age of seventy-seven.
L6Y9-CQ6: Abraham Odom Sr. was born in 1705 and made his life in colonial America. He died in 1771 in Granville, North Carolina, at about sixty-six years of age.
LZLC-DJ8: Anne Michel was born in 1648 and emigrated to New France, arriving in 1668 as a fille du roi, one of the king's daughters sponsored by the crown to marry colonists and help populate the colony. She settled along the St. Lawrence and died in 1724 at Contrecoeur near Montreal.
LTTK-QT4: John Godfrey was born in 1680 in Newport, Rhode Island, and lived his short life in that colonial seaport. He died in Newport in 1709, at about twenty-nine years of age.
LRN5-QC3: Abigail Austin was born in 1695 in Charlestown, Massachusetts, and spent her entire life in that Middlesex County town. She died there in 1747 at the age of about fifty-two.
L6YQ-M9B: John James Gilliland was born in 1689 in Londonderry, Ireland. In 1724 he immigrated to America, arriving in Maryland, and he eventually settled at Faggs Manor in Londonderry Township, Chester County, Pennsylvania, where he died in 1727.
L2QT-RGB: William Ancell Carter III was born in 1591 in Kempston, Bedfordshire, England, and lived his life in that English county. He died in 1634 at Campton, Bedfordshire, at about forty-three years of age.
GJT3-YL1: John Stackhouse was born in 1638 in Yorkshire, England, and is recorded as having died in 1670 at Giggleswick in the same county. Records also note an arrival in 1682 at New Castle, Delaware, in the American colonies.
9KDT-6ZC: Jannetje Marie Jane Varleth was born in 1621 in Utrecht, in the Netherlands, and crossed the Atlantic to the American colonies. She died in 1674 at Chesapeake City in Cecil County, Maryland, where her grave is memorialized.
LB7N-GN3: Mary Stockbridge was born in 1655 in Scituate, then part of Plymouth Colony in Massachusetts. She later journeyed south to South Carolina, where she died in Charleston in 1696 at about forty-one years of age.
M5Z1-RCL: Elizabeth Framington was born in 1669 in Topsfield, Massachusetts. She lived in the Bay Colony and died in 1747 at Framingham in Middlesex County, at the age of about seventy-eight.
G8XB-F27: Georg Breh was born in 1623 in the village of Vogelbach in Baden-Württemberg, Germany, and worked as a charcoal burner, or Köhler. He died in 1701 at nearby Lütschenbach, having spent his life in the forested hills of the Lörrach district.
M42J-VN7: Angelin Arlu was born in France in 1652 and lived along the southern French coast. He died in 1694 at Cannes in the Alpes-Maritimes, at about forty-two years of age.
M42J-VNB: Jean Sauvestre was born in France in 1630 and lived his entire life there, dying in 1690 at the age of about sixty.
M6LT-H8M: Hanss Michael Sanwald was born in 1665 at Uttenhofen in Württemberg, Germany. He died in 1713 at Raibach in the Dieburg district of Hesse, at about forty-eight years of age.
9QX3-F2C: Hanss Jacob Koppenhoeffer was born in 1638 at Uttenhofen in Württemberg, Germany. He married on 27 November 1655 at Westheim, and he was buried there on 6 May 1694, having spent his life in that corner of Württemberg.
